lessons.title: Operations on Real Numbers
concepts: Real numbers include rational and irrational numbers.
BODMAS: Brackets, Orders, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction.

experiment.description: Order of Operations Challenge: Solve a series of expressions where you must click the operations in the correct order to simplify the expression.

lessons.title: Fractions, Decimals, and Percentages
objectives: Convert between fractions, decimals, and percentages
concepts: Fractions represent parts of a whole.
Percentages are parts per hundred.

experiment.description: Visual Fraction/Percentage Slider: A bar that you can slide to see how a fraction (e.g., 3/4) converts to a decimal (0.75) and a percentage (75%) visually.

lessons.title: Algebraic Expressions and Equations
concepts: Like terms can be added or subtracted.
Balancing equations: whatever you do to one side, you must do to the other.

experiment.description: Equation Balance Scale: Drag constants and variables (x) onto a balance scale to solve for x by keeping the scale balanced.

lessons.title: Quadratic Equations
concepts: Standard form: ax^2 + bx + c = 0.
The quadratic formula: x = [-b ± sqrt(b^2 - 4ac)] / 2a

experiment.description: Parabola Explorer: Adjust sliders for a, b, and c in ax^2 + bx + c to see how the parabola shifts, widens, or narrows on a graph.

lessons.title: Linear Inequalities
objectives: Solve linear inequalities in one variable
Represent solutions on a number line
concepts: Inequality symbols: <, >, ≤, ≥.
Multiplying or dividing by a negative number flips the inequality sign.

experiment.description: Inequality Mapper: Input an inequality (e.g., 2x + 3 > 7) and watch the solution shade automatically on an interactive number line.

lessons.title: Coordinate Geometry
objectives: Find the gradient of a straight line
Determine the equation of a straight line
concepts: Gradient (m) = (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1).
Equation of a line: y = mx + c.

experiment.description: Line Lab: Plot y=mx+b with draggable sliders for m and b. Show the line updating in real-time with the equation displayed.

lessons.title: Introduction to Functions
concepts: A function maps an input to a unique output.
Inverse function f^-1(x) reverses the mapping.

experiment.description: Function Machine: Drop a number into a 'machine' that applies f(x) and g(x) to show composite functions visually.

lessons.title: Pythagoras' Theorem
objectives: Apply Pythagoras' theorem to find missing sides of right-angled triangles
concepts: In a right-angled triangle: a^2 + b^2 = c^2, where c is the hypotenuse.

experiment.description: Pythagoras Visualizer: Drag the vertices of a right-angled triangle and see the squares of the sides (a^2, b^2, c^2) update in real-time to satisfy the theorem.

lessons.title: Trigonometric Ratios (SOH CAH TOA)
objectives: Define sine, cosine, and tangent ratios
Use trig ratios to find angles and sides
concepts: SOH: Sin = Opposite / Hypotenuse
CAH: Cos = Adjacent / Hypotenuse
TOA: Tan = Opposite / Adjacent

experiment.description: Unit Circle Explorer: Move a point around a circle to see how the sine and cosine values relate to the coordinates of the point.

lessons.title: Introduction to Vectors
concepts: Vectors have magnitude and direction.
Addition follows the triangle or parallelogram law.

experiment.description: Vector Tug-of-War: Drag two vectors (arrows) on a grid to see their resultant vector update as they are added.

lessons.title: Matrices and Determinants
objectives: Calculate the determinant and inverse of a 2x2 matrix
concepts: Matrix multiplication: row by column.
Determinant of [a b; c d] is ad - bc.

experiment.description: Shape Shifter: Apply a 2x2 matrix to a square on a grid and watch the square undergo rotation, scaling, or shearing based on the matrix values.

lessons.title: Measures of Central Tendency
objectives: Calculate mean, median, and mode for raw and grouped data
concepts: Mean: sum of values / number of values.
Median: middle value when sorted.
Mode: most frequent value.

experiment.description: Live Stats: Add/remove data points in a set and watch the mean, median, and mode indicators shift on a number line.

lessons.title: Basic Probability
objectives: Calculate the probability of a simple event
concepts: P(E) = Number of favorable outcomes / Total outcomes.
Sum of probabilities of all possible outcomes is 1.

experiment.description: Coin Flip Lab: Simulate flipping a coin 100 or 1000 times to see how the experimental probability approaches 0.5 (theoretical).

lessons.title: Perimeter and Area
objectives: Calculate perimeter and area of basic shapes
concepts: Area of rectangle = length x width.
Area of circle = πr^2.

experiment.description: Shape Flex: Resize a rectangle or circle and watch the area and perimeter values update dynamically as the dimensions change.

lessons.title: Volume and Capacity
objectives: Calculate the volume of prisms and cylinders
concepts: Volume = Area of base x height.

experiment.description: Water Tank: Fill different shaped 3D containers (prism, cylinder) with water to visualize how volume relates to base area and height.
